_description: Project-level architectural rules. Each rule is a named check run by the design-conformance gate (reference implementation: sworn designaudit) against the slice's diff. Rules can be suppressed per-release via architecture-overrides.json. Check types: grep (regex), touchpoints (planned-file audit), diff-size (growth limits), external (SAST/lint tool invocation).

canonical_docs._description: Canonical architectural source-of-truth documents. The planner consults these during discovery. The architecture audit checks conformance. Declare paths relative to the repo root. Empty arrays = not yet defined — the planner will flag this as a gap.

rules.description: Files changed shall be declared in spec.json planned touchpoints
rules.note: If intentional, add to spec.json planned touchpoints or declare in proof.json divergence

rules.description: No single file shall grow by more than 200 lines in one slice
rules.note: Large additions may indicate monolithic code dumps — split into smaller composable units
Files over 500 lines should be decomposed

rules.description: No secrets, keys, or credentials shall appear in source code
rules.note: Secrets detected in diff. Use environment variables or a secrets manager.

rules.description: SQL queries shall use parameterised queries, not string concatenation
rules.note: SQL injection risk. Use parameterised queries ($1, ?, :name) instead of string interpolation.

rules.description: Interactive elements shall have accessible labels
rules.note: Interactive element without aria-label or data-testid. Add an accessible label.

rules.description: Colour shall not be the only means of conveying information
rules.note: Colour-only status indicator. WCAG 1.4.1: ensure an additional indicator accompanies the colour.

rules.description: Code shall be understandable to a new team member 12 months from now
rules.note: LLM check for naming clarity, separation of concerns, extension surface, duplication, god objects.
